trigger: anima_audio.hexa _file_bytes() failure on remote Linux runner during ω-audio empirical (2026-04-26 session)

root_cause.primary: stat -f %z is BSD-Darwin format syntax; on Linux/GNU, -f means '--filesystem' and dumps FS metadata to stdout — to_int(out) parses 'File: ...; ID: ...; Type: ext2/ext3' and crashes
root_cause.secondary: date +%s%N is GNU date; macOS BSD date treats %N as literal 'N' suffix, returning '1234567890N' which fails to_int

interpreter_binary_baked_in.verdict: interpreter itself has BSD `stat -f '%m|%z'` hardcoded — bug not just in user code

spread.stdlib_pattern_seen: date +%s%N 2>/dev/null || date +%s — graceful but loses ns precision on Mac

concrete_failures.manifestation: ω-audio empirical run failed at to_int parse: 'File: ...; ID: ...; Type: ext2/ext3' (Linux remote runner)
Mac local: returns '1234567890N' literal — to_int falls through fallback '0' so all timing is broken silently
self-aware: tries BSD then GNU. correct pattern but adopted only here

remediation_options.approach: stdlib helper fn file_size(path: string) -> int
remediation_options.implementation: uname-detect, branch BSD vs GNU. all callers use stdlib helper

remediation_options.tradeoff: wc -c reads whole file (slower for large WAVs); stat is metadata-only O(1)

remediation_options.approach: interpreter native fn (file_size builtin in hexa.real)
remediation_options.implementation: syscall stat directly in interpreter, expose as builtin
remediation_options.cost: medium — touches Mach-O binary, requires rebuild
remediation_options.benefit: eliminates exec() overhead, ~50ms saved per call

remediation_options.tradeoff: python startup ~20ms — too slow for nanosecond-anchor uniqueness

verdict: PORTABILITY_BUG_CONFIRMED severity=HIGH; affects all hexa execution that touches file size/timestamps on dual-platform setup. 864 + 743 sites need migration.
